feature: Send iOS/web chat turn (enqueue to OpenClaw)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to send a chat message from the iOS/web app so that OpenClaw processes it and I can poll for the reply.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/chat/messages/ requires JWT auth. Body: {text, client_msg_id (optional), thread_id (optional)}. text is validated: empty→400, >8000 chars→400. client_msg_id max 64 chars→400 if exceeded; auto-generated UUID hex if absent. Idempotency: if client_msg_id already exists for the tenant, returns the existing AppChatMessage with HTTP 200 (no re-enqueue). Budget gate via check_budget: over-budget tenant creates an AppChatMessage with status=error, error='budget_exhausted', returns 200 (not created=True). Thread resolved from thread_id or defaults to the shared main thread (get_or_create race-safe); invalid thread_id→404. A fresh PendingMessage (channel=ios) is created, user text is decorated with datetime context + chat-context marker, then enqueued to QStash drain task. Returns 201 on new turn, 200 on replay. tenant.first_message_at stamped on first-ever message (conditional UPDATE, race-safe). Cache-Control: no-store on all responses.
dependencies: QStash (drain task scheduling), Postgres (AppChatMessage, PendingMessage, ChatThread), apps/billing/services:check_budget
inventory_notes: Idempotency check runs BEFORE thread validation: a retry with same client_msg_id but stale thread_id still replays 200. Budget-exhausted turns are recorded as ERROR immediately; no container is woken.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to poll a pending chat turn so that I can display the assistant's reply when it lands.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/chat/messages/<client_msg_id>/ requires JWT auth. Returns the AppChatMessage serialized as {client_msg_id, thread_id, status, source, user_text, reply_text, error, created_at, replied_at, waking_at, phase, phase_detail}. status is one of pending/ready/error. 404 if no row exists for this tenant+client_msg_id. Cache-Control: no-store to prevent HTTP caches from serving a stale 'pending' body after the reply lands (ETagMiddleware default of max-age=10 would otherwise add up to 10s of perceived latency).
inventory_notes: waking_at is non-null while a hibernated container is booting — clients show 'assistant is waking up' copy. phase/phase_detail provide live agent-activity narration while status=pending.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to fetch a unified chronological stream of all chat turns so that I can display messages from every channel (iOS, Telegram, LINE, cron) in one view.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/chat/messages/?since=<cursor>&limit=<n> requires JWT auth. Unions AppChatMessage (channel=app), ConversationTurn (telegram/line), and ProactiveOutbound (cron) for the tenant. Each stored turn is split into up to two message rows (user + assistant); ProactiveOutbound emits one assistant row. Cursor is base64(json([created_at_iso, id])); absent/malformed cursor restarts from epoch (fail-open, iOS dedups by id). limit clamped to MAX_PAGE_SIZE=100. Returns {messages: [...], cursor: <next>}. Each message row: {id, role, text, created_at, source, thread_id, client_msg_id?}. client_msg_id is echoed on both halves of device-originated AppChatMessage turns for client-side dedup by (client_msg_id, role). ConversationTurn/ProactiveOutbound rows are mapped to the tenant's shared main thread. Empty page echoes the incoming cursor unchanged. Cache-Control: no-store.
inventory_notes: Strictly-monotonic keyset cursor (created_at, id) is replica-safe. Backdated on-device turns (from ChatLocalTurnView) may fall behind an already-served watermark and be skipped — benign since those rows are device-local. Boundary-cluster logic ensures a same-microsecond burst is never truncated mid-cluster.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to list and create named chat threads so that I can have separate topic-focused conversations.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/chat/threads/ — JWT auth; ensures the shared main thread exists (get_or_create, race-safe via UniqueConstraint on is_main per tenant), then returns all threads for the tenant as [{id, title, is_main, created_at, last_active_at}]. POST /api/v1/chat/threads/ — creates a named thread (is_main=False, title truncated to 120 chars), returns 201 with the serialized thread. 404 if user has no tenant. Cache-Control: no-store on GET.
inventory_notes: The main thread (is_main=True) is guaranteed one-per-tenant by a partial UniqueConstraint. Every channel (Telegram, LINE, iOS) resumes the main thread. OpenClaw user param for a thread is 'thread:<uuid>' so each thread has its own transcript while USER.md/memory stays shared.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to load a conversation thread's recent history so that I can see past turns when opening a thread.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/chat/threads/<thread_id>/messages/ — JWT auth. thread_id must be a UUID belonging to the tenant; invalid/unknown→404. Returns newest 50 AppChatMessage rows, reversed to oldest-first for app append-in-order display. Response: {thread: {...}, messages: [...]} where each message is the full AppChatMessage serialization including waking_at, phase, phase_detail. Cache-Control: no-store.
inventory_notes: Limit is hardcoded at _HISTORY_LIMIT=50 with no cursor pagination on this endpoint. Pending turns (no reply yet) are included with status=pending.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to fetch a compact markdown snapshot of my personal data so that my on-device model can answer questions without sending data to a cloud provider.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/chat/context/?max_chars=<n> — JWT auth with per-hour rate throttle (ChatContextHourThrottle). max_chars clamped to [CONTEXT_DIGEST_MIN_CHARS, CONTEXT_DIGEST_MAX_CHARS], defaults to CONTEXT_DIGEST_DEFAULT_CHARS. Renders a USER.md-like digest via render_context_digest (goals, tasks, fuel, finance, recent journal, conversation digest). PII placeholders are rehydrated to real names using the tenant's entity map before the response leaves (fail-open: on rehydration error, placeholder-space text is served). Returns {context_md, max_chars, generated_at}. Cache-Control: no-store.
dependencies: Postgres/Supabase (journal, tasks, fuel, finance models), apps/pii/redactor:rehydrate_text
inventory_notes: This is the privacy boundary: inference stays on-device; only structured data flows down. PII is rehydrated here (unlike USER.md, which is consumed inside the tenant container's placeholder pipeline).

user_story: As a subscriber using private/on-device mode, I want to record a completed on-device turn so that my assistant (crons, other channels) stays aware of our conversation.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/chat/turns/ — JWT auth with per-hour rate throttle (ChatLocalTurnHourThrottle). Body: {text, reply_text (optional), client_msg_id (optional), occurred_at (optional ISO8601), thread_id (optional)}. text truncated to 8000 chars (not rejected) then empty→400. reply_text truncated to 16000. client_msg_id max 64→400. Idempotent on client_msg_id: returns existing 200. Resolves thread or defaults to main. Creates AppChatMessage with status=READY, source=ON_DEVICE, replied_at=occurred_at or now. occurred_at backdates created_at via update() for digest accuracy (fail-open: naive/too-old/too-future dates ignored). On success triggers debounced USER.md refresh (schedule_user_md_refresh) so crons see the on-device turn. Returns 201 on create, 200 on idempotent replay.
inventory_notes: No OpenClaw container turn is enqueued (reply was produced on-device). occurred_at window: up to 35 days old and 5 minutes into the future. Backdated rows behind an already-served ?since= cursor are skipped by the feed (benign; device holds them locally).

user_story: As the OpenClaw runtime, I want to POST progress events so that polling clients can display live agent-activity narration instead of a static spinner.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/internal/runtime/<tenant_id>/chat/progress/ — no JWT; authenticated via X-NBHD-Internal-Key + X-NBHD-Tenant-Id headers (validate_internal_runtime_request). Body: {client_msg_id (optional), phase (required, max 24 chars), detail (optional, max 200 chars)}. Missing phase→400. Updates AppChatMessage.phase + phase_detail only for rows with status=PENDING for this tenant. If client_msg_id supplied, filters to that specific row; otherwise updates the most-recent PENDING row (per-tenant serialization means one in-flight app turn at a time). Missing/finished turns are a 200 no-op (late events never resurrect completed turns). Returns {updated: true|false}.
inventory_notes: Best-effort narration — only PENDING turns are updated. A Telegram/LINE turn creates no AppChatMessage so this is a no-op for those channels, which is correct. phase values: '', 'waking', 'thinking', 'tool', 'composing'.

feature: Pending message queue — enqueue and drain (per-tenant serialization)
user_story: As the system, I want to serialize inbound messages per (tenant, channel, channel_user_id) so that the OpenClaw container never receives overlapping turns on the same session.
expected_behaviour: enqueue_message_for_tenant() creates a PendingMessage row and publishes a QStash drain task (retries=3). drain_pending_messages_for_tenant_task() claims the next deliverable batch via SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED with a soft delivery_in_flight_until lease. Batch composition: head row must be under attempts cap (3) and not stale (>600s); subsequent rows folded in if also fresh, under cap, and non-voice; voice rows are always singletons. Batch POSTs to tenant.container_fqdn /v1/chat/completions with model='openclaw'. On success all batch rows are marked DELIVERED, usage recorded, reply relayed (channel-specific). On failure per-row delivery_attempts incremented; past-cap rows dropped with user apology. Stale rows dropped with user apology (for LINE/TG: channel push; for iOS: AppChatMessage→ERROR + APNs error push). OpenRouter 402/credit-limit triggers budget circuit breaker (hibernate + email + user notification). Re-schedules self if more pending rows remain.
dependencies: QStash (drain task, retries), Postgres (PendingMessage), OpenClaw container HTTP, apps/billing/services:record_usage
inventory_notes: Lease factor 1.5x timeout prevents concurrent drain tasks from overlapping. Boot grace window (240s) prevents burning delivery_attempts during slow cold starts. Stale-message guard (10 min) prevents 'responding to questions from hours ago' UX bug.

user_story: As the system, I want to detect and republish drain tasks for stuck PendingMessage rows so that messages are never silently dropped when QStash delivery fails.
expected_behaviour: reap_stuck_inbound_messages_task() runs every ~60s via QStash cron. Scans for PENDING rows older than 90s with no live in-flight lease. Groups by (tenant, channel, channel_user_id) key, up to 200 keys per tick. For each stuck key publishes a fresh drain task (retries=3). The drain task handles concurrency cleanly via SKIP LOCKED. Covers three failure modes: (1) publish_task raised at enqueue time, (2) QStash DLQ after all retries exhausted, (3) gunicorn worker died mid-claim. Logs a warning only when stuck keys exist (silent on clean ticks).
dependencies: QStash (cron schedule + drain task republish), Postgres (PendingMessage)
inventory_notes: Reaper does NOT process messages itself — only republishes drain tasks. The drain's stale-age guard prevents delivery of 7-hour-old messages after a reaper-triggered drain. Batch cap (200 keys/tick) prevents budget overruns at fleet scale.

feature: iOS drain — reply persistence and APNs push
user_story: As the system, I want to persist the OpenClaw reply to AppChatMessage and push an APNs notification so that the iOS client can read the reply immediately without polling forever.
expected_behaviour: _drain_ios_batch() POSTs the batch to the container (model='openclaw', X-Channel: ios). On healthy reply: PII is rehydrated, insight markers extracted + recorded, [[chart:]] / MEDIA: markers stripped, AppChatMessage rows updated to status=READY with the clean reply_text. APNs push ('reply ready') is dispatched in a daemon thread (non-blocking) via notify_app_reply_ready. Idempotent: atomic notified_at claim prevents double-push on QStash retry/re-lease. On empty/gateway-error reply: status→ERROR, error='empty_response', APNs 'couldn't finish' push fired. On OpenRouter 402: budget circuit breaker triggered, AppChatMessage→ERROR with error='budget_exhausted'. APNs tokens pruned on 410 (unregistered) responses.
dependencies: OpenClaw container HTTP, Postgres (AppChatMessage, DeviceToken), APNs (apps/common/apns), apps/pii/redactor, apps/insights/markers
inventory_notes: Push is dispatched off the drain path (daemon thread) so a slow APNs send never holds the per-key lease. The apns-collapse-id (client_msg_id) and notified_at claim provide belt-and-suspenders idempotency.

user_story: As the system, I want to detect a hibernated container when drain POST returns 404 or connection error and wake it without burning the delivery_attempts budget.
expected_behaviour: In drain_pending_messages_for_tenant_task: if the POST raises an httpx 404 / ConnectError / ConnectTimeout AND tenant.hibernated_at is not None AND budget check passes, the drain: (a) releases the lease without incrementing delivery_attempts, (b) wakes the container via wake_hibernated_tenant(), (c) sends a 'waking up, hold on' notification (Telegram only; LINE already acked; iOS gets waking_at stamped on AppChatMessage), (d) reschedules the drain with _WAKE_DEFER_SECONDS=20 delay. Boot grace window: if the container was woken within 240s but still returns 404, the lease is released again (no attempt burn) and drain retried — prevents burning the 3-attempt cap during a slow cold start. Over-budget hibernated tenants fall through to the normal attempt-cap path (not re-woken).
dependencies: Postgres (Tenant.hibernated_at, Tenant.last_wake_at), Azure Container Apps, apps/billing/services:check_budget
inventory_notes: The Telegram poller path enqueues directly to PendingMessage (no hibernation check unlike the webhook path), so this drain-level wake is the only safety net for poller-sourced messages to hibernated containers. A healthy drain response also clears a stale hibernated_at flag set by the poller.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to register my iOS device token so that the server can push 'reply ready' notifications to my device.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/push/register/ — JWT auth. Body: {device_token (hex string 32–200 chars), environment ('sandbox'|'production', defaults to 'production'), bundle_id (optional, max 128 chars)}. Invalid token format (non-hex or wrong length)→400. Upsert via update_or_create on token (globally unique): re-points device to the registering user atomically on account switch or reinstall. Returns {registered: true} 200. DELETE /api/v1/push/register/?device_token=<token> (or in body) — deletes the token for request.user only. Returns 204. Missing token→400.
inventory_notes: Token global uniqueness means registration is a safe upsert: a device switched to a new account overwrites the old row rather than creating a duplicate. Tokens APNs reports as unregistered (410) are self-pruned by the push paths.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to send a test push notification to my own devices so that I can verify notifications are working end-to-end.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/push/test/ — JWT auth, rate-limited (PushTestMinuteThrottle). If APNs is not configured returns {sent:0, failed:0, skipped:'not_configured'} 200. If user has no registered tokens returns {sent:0, failed:0, skipped:'no_tokens'} 200. Sends a FIXED static body ('Test push — notifications are working.') to all of request.user's registered tokens, routing each to the correct APNs host (sandbox vs production). Returns {sent, failed, unregistered}. Stale tokens (APNs 410) pruned. Push body is always static — never contains any user/tenant data.
inventory_notes: Security: can only push to request.user's own tokens; token is never accepted from the request body. APNs 200 means Apple accepted the push, not that the device displayed it — this endpoint lets users verify the last hop.

user_story: As the system, I want to push an APNs notification when a cron or proactive message is delivered so that iOS-only users are notified without Telegram/LINE.
expected_behaviour: notify_proactive_ready() is called from record_proactive_outbound() (off the request path via daemon thread). Atomic notified_at claim on ProactiveOutbound ensures a single push even if the function is called multiple times for the same row. Resolves tenant.user's registered devices, routes each to sandbox/production APNs host. Push body is a 140-char preview of the proactive message text, with markdown stripped and table detection (tables get generic body to avoid lock-screen leakage). Collapse-id is 'cron:<proactive_id>'. Stale tokens (APNs 410) self-pruned. No-op if APNs unconfigured. Fail-open.
inventory_notes: The push is a wake-and-sync trigger only; authoritative text reaches the app via the ?since= feed. Dispatched in a daemon thread off the CronDeliveryView request path to avoid blocking the cron tool-call response.

user_story: As the system, I want to prepend prior proactive/cron outbounds to each inbound message so that the assistant can anchor multi-paragraph user replies to the original questions.
expected_behaviour: surface_proactive_context() called by LINE webhook, Telegram webhook, and Telegram poller for each inbound. Queries ProactiveOutbound rows for (tenant, channel, channel_user_id) within 24h, newest-first, limit 3. Keeps unconsumed rows AND consumed-but-recent rows (within 5-min follow-up window). First-time-surfaced rows are marked consumed_at atomically. Renders an '[earlier-from-you <datetime> <job>:\n<text>\n]' block (oldest-first). If any row has parsed_items (bullet/numbered list), prepends a [thread-rule:] guidance for the agent to map reply paragraphs by index. Returns empty string if nothing to surface.
inventory_notes: consumed_at is deliberately re-surfaced within the 5-min follow-up window so 'thanks, then real answer' sequences still see the context. parsed_items extraction (parse_markdown_items) requires ≥2 list items to constitute a structure.

user_story: As the OpenClaw runtime, I want to send proactive or cron messages to the user via Django so that the correct channel (Telegram, LINE, or iOS app) is used and thread continuity is maintained.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/internal/runtime/<tenant_id>/send/ (mounted via integrations/urls.py). Auth: X-NBHD-Internal-Key + X-NBHD-Tenant-Id. tenant.status != ACTIVE→200 {status:'blocked'} (no retry). Channel resolution: prefers user.preferred_channel if linked; fallbacks: any linked messaging channel; iOS app (DeviceToken exists) as last resort. No delivery surface→422. Rate limit: 20 messages/hour per tenant (in-process memory, resets on restart). Body: {message, parse_mode (Markdown|HTML|plain, default Markdown)}. PII rehydration before send. Telegram: markdown→Telegram HTML (render_telegram_html), HTML fallback to plain text on 400; LINE: push API. channel='app': only APNs + ?since= feed (no chat message sent). On success records ProactiveOutbound row, dispatches iOS APNs push. X-NBHD-Job-Name header is captured in ProactiveOutbound.job_name.
dependencies: Telegram Bot API, LINE Messaging API, Postgres (ProactiveOutbound, DeviceToken), APNs, apps/pii/redactor
inventory_notes: Rate limit is in-process dict — resets on deploy/restart. iOS-only users ('app' channel) receive the message ONLY via the ?since= feed and APNs; the text is stored in ProactiveOutbound, never in AppChatMessage.

user_story: As the system, I want to persist every completed Telegram/LINE turn to ConversationTurn so that cron sessions can see today's conversation without the user explicitly journaling.
expected_behaviour: record_conversation_turn() called fail-open from _drain_telegram_batch(), _drain_line_batch(), and the Telegram webhook after each successful reply relay. Creates a ConversationTurn row with channel, channel_user_id, local_date (tenant-tz date), user_text (≤2000 chars), reply_text (PII-rehydrated + marker-stripped, ≤800 chars). Probabilistically (1% chance per call) prunes expired rows (>35 days) in batches of 500. Triggers debounced USER.md push (schedule_user_md_refresh) with 120s debounce via on_commit + daemon thread. AppChatMessage (iOS) is NOT captured here — the digest reads that table directly to avoid double-storage.
inventory_notes: Fail-open: a lost ConversationTurn row never 500s the user reply. Debounce prevents storming the Azure File Share on high-frequency chat. The digest renders today's turns (last 6, 130-char one-liners) + 3-day rollup.
